text.title: Project Closure Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the project-closure methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Each deliverable MUST have written acceptance (signature/email/system entry) before closure declared.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Lessons-learned session MUST run BEFORE team disbands; post-disband capture is fiction.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: All contracts (vendor + customer) MUST be closed (final invoice + retention release) before closure.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Handover doc MUST be signed by receiving ops team — silent hand-off is not hand-off.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Archive MUST contain a manifest (file list + locations) so artefacts are retrievable years later.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for cancelled projects (use cancellation playbook) or open-ended programs.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
